title: Angels and Evolution
wikitext:
[]: Angels and Evolution             Quote Post by kylethestrange » Thu Oct 25, 2012 12:15 pm I'm sure I'm not the only one to wonder, why do angels not have to evolve fire millions of years like we did? Is that one of the advantages of the FOL. And the FOK makes us have to evolve? The CI in NGE2 says the FAR are humanoid. So why would.Adam and Lilith be humanoid, but as her children we didn't start as lilim. We had to evolve.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Thu Oct 25, 2012 12:29 pm Humans didn't evolve in the Evaverse. They were grown directly from Lilith, I believe. (Note the tiny legs growing from her while she's on the cross.)

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Oct 25, 2012 2:53 pm No, he's right. We did evolve out of LCL. The reason is probably because humans can't just EXIST. We need an ecosystem so we can eat things, breathe air, and not be savages in caves. We don't have super S2 Engine god powers, so we need dominion over the fertile Earth.

[]:             Quote Post by Pandymint » Thu Oct 25, 2012 3:39 pm LCL is more or less the primordial soup that life came from in the beginning of the Earth. So I suppose you could say LCL itself is the Lilin, and if you take that into account we are just the end of LCL's Evolutionary Cycle. All of the Angels are possible evolutions, if I recall correctly, that's why humans are considered the 18th Angel. And for all we know, the Angels spent billions of years lying dormant, evolving, what have you. And they just somehow managed to reach their final forms around the same time.

[]:             Quote Post by tootiredtoriot » Thu Oct 25, 2012 6:22 pm maybe if we had laid dormant a bit longer and didn't have such a short pregnancy / lifespan we would have evolved into something a bit more impressive too

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Thu Oct 25, 2012 9:01 pm AuraTwilight wrote: No, he's right. We did evolve out of LCL. The reason is probably because humans can't just EXIST. We need an ecosystem so we can eat things, breathe air, and not be savages in caves. We don't have super S2 Engine god powers, so we need dominion over the fertile Earth.

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Thu Oct 25, 2012 9:09 pm I was considering Sandalphon and how quickly it evolved in the volcano. Was this proven to be a one time occurrence for this angel or do you think all angels evolved this quickly from their embryonic state?

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Oct 25, 2012 9:22 pm Lurkis wrote: I was considering Sandalphon and how quickly it evolved in the volcano. Was this proven to be a one time occurrence for this angel or do you think all angels evolved this quickly from their embryonic state?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 26, 2012 1:10 am Merkaba wrote: I thought for sure I read an analysis on this site talking about how the first humans literally grew from Lilith, which explained the tiny legs growing from her. :shrugs:

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Fri Oct 26, 2012 3:37 pm Reichu wrote: The Angels start out as "human", and they "reject human form". "Imagining yourself in your own heart", taken to its logical conclusion?

[]:             Quote Post by Pandymint » Fri Oct 26, 2012 3:40 pm Reichu wrote: The Angels start out as "human", and they "reject human form". "Imagining yourself in your own heart", taken to its logical conclusion?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 26, 2012 4:06 pm Lurkis wrote: Where did you hear that the angels started out in human form then rejected it? I would love to read it ^^

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Oct 26, 2012 4:17 pm Reichu wrote: The Angels start out as "human", and they "reject human form". "Imagining yourself in your own heart", taken to its logical conclusion?

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Fri Oct 26, 2012 4:18 pm I didnt think Misato was speaking literally about Angels rejecting human form and becoming what they are. I interpreted it more as a 'we all come from the same place (FAR) and they are, in that sense, what could have spawned here in our place.' I can't imagine them rejecting humanity since angels would never have had that chance to begin with, being from the fruit of life.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 26, 2012 4:35 pm Lurkis wrote: I didnt think Misato was speaking literally about Angels rejecting human form and becoming what they are.

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Fri Oct 26, 2012 4:39 pm Reichu wrote: The Fruit of Life is just the S2 Engine. In a sense, it's freedom from limitations, and perhaps that freedom is what causes Angels to seize at whatever forms they can imagine and leave human form behind.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Fri Oct 26, 2012 4:52 pm Is this to possibly say that Tabris consciously chose to take human form? Or is it simply because of the DNA of Lilim and Angel meeting at Second Impact?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 26, 2012 4:54 pm Lurkis: A humanoid Angel would probably look like Adam or an Eva, given their genetic background. As for knowledge and capabilities, it's hard to say. The "power" of the Lilin is born of its limitations -- physical weakness, mortality, etc. A humanoid Angel would still have the Fruit of Life and thus be exempt from most traditionally "human" motivators. Food, drink, etc.? All such needs are supplied by the S2 Engine. Shelter? They can't build better protection than their A.T. Field. Reproduction? If you live forever it's not an immediate concern. Weapons? They're masters of psychokinesis, and shapeshifters to boot; they supply their own. And so on.

[]:             Quote Post by kylethestrange » Sat Oct 27, 2012 11:23 am Just on the evolution topic, are the angels also against each other? In a survival of the fittest way? For example, if Sachiel would have made it to Adam, would it have wiped out all the other angels and the planet would have been all Sachiels? Or do angels, since they are basically immortal, not reproduce and it would have just been the one Sachiel. I just wonder because of Kaworu fought other angels. But then again I figure possessing a human body would produce conflicts within him. Since the body I thought was implied to be an actual humans with Adam's soul implanted.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Oct 27, 2012 11:46 am kylethestrange: No two Angels ever meet one another (unless Israfel counts), so it's unclear how they would behave if they did . The Classified Information paints the Angel vs. Lilin conflict as a battle of survival in and of itself, so, quite possibly, the Angels would be open to working together -- so long as they have a greater enemy, in us. But if that threat were removed, they would be under no greater obligation to get along than Lilin would be under similar circumstances. If there's anything about humanity that's a constant, it's their ability to find reasons to reject one another.

Is that one of the advantages of the FOL. And the FOK makes us have to evolve? The CI in NGE2 says the FAR are humanoid. So why would.Adam and Lilith be humanoid, but as her children we didn't start as lilim. We had to evolve.
